title: Competency Question Suite
description: Gold CQ sets: fixed questions with verifiable expected answers. expected_sql is the human-reviewed truth; wrong_sql (optional) is a known-wrong formulation whose result MUST differ (canary). The CQ runner executes both against the built fixture.

expected_kind.description: refusal: a conforming consumer must refuse (e.g. fan-out without strategy, deprecated-only path). clarification: must ask, not guess (ambiguous intent).

expected_sql.description: Human-reviewed SQL producing the expected answer. Required for scalar/rows.

wrong_sql.description: Known-wrong formulation (naive fan-out sum, missing required filter...). Runner asserts its result differs materially from expected_sql.

references.description: Semantic-layer objects this CQ exercises (metric/table/hierarchy refs). Used for per-element consumer coverage.

trap.description: For canary CQs: which failure mode wrong_sql embodies (fan_out, missing_required_filter, snapshot_overcount, current_row_scd_join, deprecated_table, wrong_grain_aggregate).
